title: How to purchase an advanced life deferred annuity (ALDA) - Canada.ca
content:
## Advanced life deferred annuity (ALDA)
- Overview Purchasing an ALDA Limits on transfers to purchase an ALDA
- Limits on transfers to purchase an ALDA
- What to do when you have an ALDA cumulative excess amount
- Waiver or cancellation of tax
- Examples of ALDA cumulative excess amounts
- Effective January 1, 2020 , the tax rules were amended to permit an ALDA to be a qualifying annuity purchase from certain registered plans. An ALDA is a life annuity where the annuity payment must be started before the end of the year in which you turn 85 years of age.
- An ALDA is a contract for a life annuity that must be issued by a licensed annuities provider.
- An ALDA is payable for as long as you live, or if it is a joint-lives annuity, for as long as you or your spouse or common-law partner lives.
For more information about transferring funds to an ALDA, go to Chapter 9 – Advanced life deferred annuity (ALDA) transfers in Guide T4040 , RRSPs and Other Registered Plans for Retirement .
To purchase an ALDA, you must directly transfer a lump-sum amount from one of the following registered plans:
- registered retirement savings plan (RRSP)
- registered retirement income fund (RRIF)
- deferred profit sharing plan (DPSP)
- pooled registered pension plan (PRPP)
- money purchase registered pension plan (MP RPP)
Follow the instructions on page 2 of the form T2157, Direct Transfer from a Registered Plan to Purchase an ALDA and give it to your licensed annuity provider.

### Limits on transfers to purchase an ALDA
If you make a transfer to purchase an ALDA, either of the following purchases may result in an ALDA cumulative excess amount which is subject to tax:
- A purchase that results in an excess ALDA transfer (informally referred to as an amount in excess of the 25% limit). Go to Chart A of the T1-OVP-ALDA, Individual Tax Return for Excess Transfers to Purchase an ALDA return to determine these amounts.
- A purchase that causes you to exceed the ALDA dollar limit for the current year (the lifetime maximum for all of your ALDA purchases). For the latest ALDA dollar limit amount, go to MP , DB, RRSP, DPSP, ALDA, TFSA limits, YMPE, and the YAMPE .
ALDA cumulative excess amounts are subject to a tax of 1% per month for every month they are left in the ALDA at the end of the month.
If your ALDA purchases are subject to tax, you have to complete and file a T1-OVP-ALDA, Individual Tax Return for Excess Transfers to Purchase an ALDA return on or before the filing due date of your income tax and benefit return.
John decides that he wants to purchase an ALDA in 2025 . At the end of 2024 , the fair market value (FMV) of his RRSP was $200,000 , and the FMV of his RRIF was $600,000 .
In February 2025 , John transfers $50,000 from his RRSP directly to the ALDA. This is the maximum amount that he can transfer from his RRSP and not create an excess ALDA transfer. In September 2025 , John wants to transfer more funds to his ALDA from his RRIF.
John thinks that he can transfer $150,000 from his RRIF as it will not create an excess ALDA transfer. However, John has forgotten that the lifetime ALDA dollar limit for 2025 is $180,000 . As a result, John will only be permitted to transfer $130,000 from his RRIF to his ALDA.

## What to do when you have an ALDA cumulative excess amount
If you have an ALDA cumulative excess amount at the end of any month for a particular year, you must file a T1-OVP-ALDA return for that year.
If you do not already have an ALDA cumulative excess amount at the start of a particular month and you make a transfer in that month that exceeds one of the ALDA transfer limits, you will not have to file a T1-OVP-ALDA return if the excess amount is refunded from the ALDA before the end of that month.
You can use the interactive T1-OVP-ALDA return to check if your purchases will result in an ALDA cumulative excess amount before you actually make your ALDA purchases.

## Waiver or cancellation of tax
If you determined that you must pay a tax on your ALDA cumulative excess amount, you may ask in writing that we waive or cancel the tax if both of the following conditions are met:
- your cumulative excess amount on which the tax is based arose due to a reasonable error
- you are taking, or have taken, reasonable steps to eliminate the cumulative excess amount
A waiver refers to the tax that is otherwise payable by a taxpayer for which relief is granted by the CRA before this amount is assessed or charged to the taxpayer. A cancellation refers to the amount of tax that was assessed or charged to the taxpayer for which relief is granted by the CRA.
To consider your request, we will need you to send a letter in which both of the following conditions are explained in detail:
- why you made the cumulative excess amount and why this is a reasonable error;
- what steps you are taking, or have taken, to eliminate the cumulative excess amount
Send your letter and supporting documents (such as copies of your ALDA account statements that identify the date you withdrew your cumulative excess amount as well as any other correspondence that shows that your cumulative excess amount arose due to a reasonable error) to:
Canada Revenue Agency Sudbury Tax Centre Pension Workflow Team Post Office Box 20000, Station A Sudbury ON  P3A 5C1
For more information on cancellation or waiver of late-filing penalties and interest, go to Information Circular IC07-1R1 , Taxpayer Relief Provisions .

## Examples of the ALDA cumulative excess amounts
On December 31, 2024 , the taxpayer has a $200,000 balance in a RRIF with financial institution A (RRIF-A), and a $100,000 balance in a RRIF with financial institution B (RRIF-B) .
Assume that apart from the transfers to an ALDA, there are no fluctuations in the value of the taxpayer’s registered plans for 2025 .
The following transfers are made under a contract for an ALDA that the taxpayer has entered into with a licensed annuities provider:
- April 15, 2025: $90,000 is transferred from RRIF-A.
- April 16, 2025: $90,000 is transferred from RRIF-B.
In September 2025, the taxpayer becomes aware that they have an ALDA cumulative excess amount, and on September 26, 2025 , $105,000 is refunded from the ALDA to the taxpayer.

### Chart A – Calculating your excess ALDA transfers for 2025 and previous years
To determine if you have any excess ALDA transfers (amounts in excess of the 25% limit), enter the information about all of your ALDA purchases for 2025 and prior years in chronological order (first purchase in the first row, second purchase in the second row, etc.).
Example 1 Chart A 2025

### Chart B – Calculating your ALDA cumulative excess amounts for 2025
Example 1 Chart B 2025
Enter the total amount of all of your ALDA transfers for all years up to the end of the month.
The ALDA dollar limit for 2025.
Line 1 minus line 2. If the result is negative, enter "0".
Enter the total amount of all of your excess ALDA transfers from all years up to the end of the month. See Chart A.
Enter the greater of line 3 and line 4.
Enter the total amount of all of the refunds from your ALDA(s) from all years up to the end of the month.
Line 5 minus line 6. If the result is negative, enter "0". This is your ALDA cumulative excess amount for the month.
Add the amounts for all months from Line 7. Enter the total on line 1 of Step 2 on page 1.
Before filling out this step, fill out Charts A and B.
Enter the amount from Line 8 in Chart B. This is the amount subject to tax.
Multiply line 1 by line 2. This is the tax on your ALDA cumulative excess amount, and your balance owing.
On December 31, 2024 , a taxpayer has a $500,000 balance in a money purchase account under an RPP, and a $320,000 balance in a RRIF with financial institution B (RRIF-B).
Assume that apart from the ALDA transfers, there are no fluctuations in the value of the taxpayer’s registered plans for 2025 . The following transfers are made under a contract for an ALDA that the taxpayer has entered into with a licensed annuities provider:
- April 15, 2025: $125,000 is transferred from MP RPP.
- June 16, 2025: $80,000 is transferred from RRIF.
In September 2025, the taxpayer becomes aware that they have an ALDA cumulative excess amount, and on September 26, 2025 , $25,000 is refunded from the ALDA to the taxpayer.

### Chart A – Calculating your excess ALDA transfers for 2025 and previous years
To determine if you have any excess ALDA transfers (amounts in excess of the 25% limit), enter the information about all of your ALDA purchases for 2025 and prior years in chronological order (first purchase in the first row, second purchase in the second row, etc.).
Example 2 chart A 2025
Enter the date of the ALDA purchase. YYYYMMDD
Enter the type of plan the amount was transferred from (for example, RRSP, RRIF).
Enter the plan number of the plan the amount was transferred from.
Enter the amount transferred from the plan to purchase an ALDA on the purchase date.
Enter the FMV of your account in the plan at the end of the year before the year of the ALDA transfer. See note Footnote 3 below.
Enter the total amount of all ALDA transfers made in previous years from this plan. Do not include any ALDA transfers made in the current year.
Column B plus column C.
Multiply column D by 25%.
Enter the total amount of all previous ALDA transfers made from this plan, including any previous ALDA transfers made in the current year.
Column E minus column F. If the result is negative, enter "0".
Column A minus column G. If the result is negative, enter "0". This is the excess ALDA transfer.

### Chart B – Calculating your ALDA cumulative excess amounts for 2025
Example 2 chart B 2025
Enter the total amount of all of your ALDA transfers for all years up to the end of the month.
The ALDA dollar limit for 2025.
Line 1 minus line 2. If the result is negative, enter "0".
Enter the total amount of all of your excess ALDA transfers from all years up to the end of the month. See Chart A.
Enter the greater of line 3 and line 4.
Enter the total amount of all of the refunds from your ALDA(s) from all years up to the end of the month.
Line 5 minus line 6. If the result is negative, enter "0". This is your ALDA cumulative excess amount for the month.
Add the amounts for all months from Line 7. Enter the total on line 1 of Step 2 on page 1.

### Calculating the amount of tax you have to pay
Before filling out this step, fill out Charts A and B.
Enter the amount from Line 8 in Chart B. This is the amount subject to tax.
Multiply line 1 by line 2. This is the tax on your ALDA cumulative excess amount, and your balance owing.
For this complex example, you will need to complete separate T1-OVP-ALDA returns for 2024 and 2025 .

### Chart A – Calculating your excess ALDA transfers for 2024 and previous years
To determine if you have any excess ALDA transfers (amounts in excess of the 25% limit), enter the information about all of your ALDA purchases for 2024 and prior years in chronological order (first purchase in the first row, second purchase in the second row, etc.).
For this example, the ALDA dollar limit for 2024 is $170,000 and the ALDA dollar limit for 2025 is $180,000 .
Example 3 chart A 2024
Enter the date of the ALDA purchase.(YYYYMMDD)
Enter the type of plan the amount was transferred from (for example, RRSP, RRIF).
Enter the plan number of the plan the amount was transferred from.
Enter the amount transferred from the plan to purchase an ALDA on the purchase date.
Enter the FMV of your account in the plan at the end of the year before the year of the ALDA transfer. See note Footnote 5 below.
Enter the total amount of all ALDA transfers made in previous years from this plan. Do not include any ALDA transfers made in the current year.
Column B plus column C.
Multiply column D by 25%.
Enter the total amount of all previous ALDA transfers made from this plan, including any previous ALDA transfers made in the current year.
Column E minus column F. If the result is negative, enter "0".
Column A minus column G. If the result is negative, enter "0". This is the excess ALDA transfer.

### Chart B – Calculating your ALDA cumulative excess amounts for 2024
Example 3 chart B 2024
Enter the total amount of all of your ALDA transfers for all years up to the end of the month.
The ALDA dollar limit for 2024.
Line 1 minus line 2. If the result is negative, enter "0"
Enter the total amount of all of your excess ALDA transfers from all years up to the end of the month. See Chart A.
Enter the greater of line 3 and line 4.
Enter the total amount of all of the refunds from your ALDA(s) from all years up to the end of the month.
Line 5 minus line 6. If the result is negative, enter "0" This is your ALDA cumulative excess amount for the month.
Add the amounts for all months from Line 7. Enter the total on line 1 of Step 2 on page 1.
Before filling out this step, fill out Charts A and B.
Enter the amount from Line 8 in Chart B for 2025. This is the amount subject to tax.
Multiply line 1 by line 2. This is the tax on your ALDA cumulative excess amount, and your balance owing.
After completing calculation for prior year, complete calculation for the current year.

### Chart A – Calculating your excess ALDA transfers for 2025 and previous years
To determine if you have any excess ALDA transfers (amounts in excess of the 25% limit), enter the information about all of your ALDA purchases for 2025 and prior years in chronological order (first purchase in the first row, second purchase in the second row, etc.).
Example 3 chart A 2025
Enter the date of the ALDA purchase. (YYYYMMDD)
Enter the type of plan the amount was transferred from (for example, RRSP, RRIF).
Enter the plan number of the plan the amount was transferred from.
Enter the amount transferred from the plan to purchase an ALDA on the purchase date.
Enter the FMV of your account in the plan at the end of the year before the year of the ALDA transfer. See note Footnote 9 below.
Enter the total amount of all ALDA transfers made in previous years from this plan. Do not include any ALDA transfers made in the current year.
Column B plus column C.
Multiply column D by 25%.
Enter the total amount of all previous ALDA transfers made from this plan, including any previous ALDA transfers made in the current year.
Column E minus column F. If the result is negative, enter "0".
Column A minus column G. If the result is negative, enter "0". This is the excess ALDA transfer.

### Chart B – Calculating your ALDA cumulative excess amounts for 2025
Example 3 chart B 2025
Enter the total amount of all of your ALDA transfers for all years up to the end of the month.
The ALDA dollar limit for 2025.
Line 1 minus line 2. If the result is negative, enter "0".
Enter the total amount of all of your excess ALDA transfers from all years up to the end of the month. See Chart A.
Enter the greater of line 3 and line 4.
Enter the total amount of all of the refunds from your ALDA(s) from all years up to the end of the month.
Line 5 minus line 6. If the result is negative, enter "0". This is your ALDA cumulative excess amount for the month.
Add the amounts for all months from Line 7. Enter the total on line 1 of Step 2 on page 1.
Note : Before filling out this step, fill out Charts A and B.
Enter the amount from Line 8 in Chart B for 2025. This is the amount subject to tax.
Multiply line 1 by line 2. This is the tax on your ALDA cumulative excess amount, and your balance owing.

## Forms and publications
- Guide T4040, RRSPs and Other Registered Plans for Retirement .
- Form T2157, Direct Transfer from a Registered Plan to Purchase an ALDA .
- Form T1-OVP-ALDA, Individual Tax Return for Excess Transfers to Purchase an ALDA .

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
